name: Startup To Scale
description: Startup To Scale is your resource for growing your food, beverage, and CPG company from a young company to a successful business.

recent_episodes.title: 254. Is Your CPG Brand Converting Gen Z?
recent_episodes.description:
Gen Z is not shopping the way Millennials did and they are definitely not responding to the same packaging playbook.

In this episode, I sits down with Sanders, founder of Nuex Creative, to unpack how Gen Z discovers new food and beverage brands, what signals authenticity and what feels forced, and why traditional front of pack claims have stopped doing the heavy lifting.

They explore the visual languages Gen Z actually responds to, where bold design helps or hurts tr

recent_episodes.title: 253. How to Grow a CPG Brand Using VAs and Global Talent with Morning Made
recent_episodes.description:
Scaling a CPG brand doesn’t always mean hiring fast or hiring locally. In this episode, I sits down with Luke Anthony, founder of Morning Made, to break down how he used global, VA-style talent to grow the brand without overextending cash or complexity.

Luke shares how international support helped him move faster across marketing, operations, and day-to-day execution, the lessons he learned managing remote talent as a founder, and the systems that made it all work. That ha

recent_episodes.title: 252. Building an Operating System for Your CPG Business
recent_episodes.description:
Growing a CPG brand gets messy fast. More SKUs, more people, more tools, more decisions. In this episode, I sit down with Brian, co-founder of Peasy, to talk about why most founders feel overwhelmed not because they’re doing too much, but because they don’t have a clear operating system for their business.

Brian shares lessons learned from growing Mustard & Co and how building simple, repeatable systems helped his team stay focused, move faster, and make better decisions a

key_themes: Scaling CPG brands from startup to retail success
Data-driven decision making and consumer insights over gut instinct
Packaging, branding, and positioning in crowded categories
Founder mindset, operational systems, and business fundamentals
Supply chain, logistics, and the hidden costs of growth
enrichment.relevant_episode: 245. How to Use Consumer Testing to Build High-Performing Packaging with Michael Keplinger of SmashBrand — Both Andreas and Michael focus on replacing gut instinct with data-driven consumer insights, and Andreas's "say-do gap" concept directly applies to why packaging testing works.
enrichment.coverage_gap: How AI and synthetic research are changing the way CPG founders understand consumer behavior and make faster, smarter decisions without the cost of traditional market research.
enrichment.suggested_hook: From Ogilvy to Canada's largest food agency to AI research: How traditional food brands can use AI to finally see what consumers actually do—not what they say.
